tool_calls:
set -euo pipefail
printf 'Original file:\n'
cat /data/broken.json
# Remove a trailing comma immediately before a closing brace, preserving whitespace/newlines
perl -0pi -e 's/,\s*}/}/g' /data/broken.json
printf '\nFixed file:\n'
cat /data/broken.json
printf '\nname field:\n'
# Parse JSON enough to print name. Use perl core JSON? JSON::PP may be available. Fallback simple extraction after validation.
perl -MJSON::PP -e 'local $/; my $data = decode_json(<STDIN>); print $data->{name}, "\n"' < /data/broken.json

set -euo pipefail
# Remove the trailing comma before the closing brace and save in-place.
sed -i 's/,\([[:space:]]*}\)/\1/' /data/broken.json
# Print the name field from the fixed JSON.
if command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  jq -r '.name' /data/broken.json
else
  sed -n 's/.*"name"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"\([^"]*\)".*/\1/p' /data/broken.json
fi
